provenance.source_corpus: Anuran call surveys on small wetlands in Prince Edward Island, Canada restored by dredging of sediments

nodes.name: dredging accumulated sediment and organic debris

source_spans.text: Wetlands were restored by means of dredging accumulated sediment and organic debris to create open water and emulate pre-disturbance conditions.
Numbers of species calling and abundance indices of northern leopard frogs, green frogs, and spring peepers were significantly higher on restored versus reference wetlands.
Numbers of species calling and abundance indices of northern leopard frogs, green frogs, and spring peepers were significantly higher on restored versus reference wetlands.
Wetlands were restored by means of dredging accumulated sediment and organic debris to create open water and emulate pre-disturbance conditions.
Numbers of species calling and abundance indices of northern leopard frogs, green frogs, and spring peepers were significantly higher on restored versus reference wetlands.

nodes.name: Increased abundance indices of northern leopard frogs

source_spans.text: Numbers of species calling and abundance indices of northern leopard frogs, green frogs, and spring peepers were significantly higher on restored versus reference wetlands.

nodes.name: Increased abundance indices of green frogs

source_spans.text: Numbers of species calling and abundance indices of northern leopard frogs, green frogs, and spring peepers were significantly higher on restored versus reference wetlands.

nodes.name: Increased abundance indices of spring peepers

source_spans.text: Numbers of species calling and abundance indices of northern leopard frogs, green frogs, and spring peepers were significantly higher on restored versus reference wetlands.
Increases in open water and water depths due to restoration also provided habitat for green frogs and northern leopard frogs.
Occurrence of calling green frogs in restored wetlands was positively correlated with percent cattail
The number of species calling in restored wetlands was positively correlated with proximity to freshwater rivers
in reference wetlands, the number was positively associated with forested perimeters and area of open water.
in reference wetlands, the number was positively associated with forested perimeters and area of open water.
in reference wetlands, with proximity to other wetlands.
Calling American toad presence was positively correlated with wetland area (component 4) and peatland habitat (component 6).
Calling American toad presence was positively correlated with wetland area (component 4) and peatland habitat (component 6).
Occurrence of calling green frogs in restored wetlands was positively correlated with percent cattail
Calling wood frog occurrence was positively correlated with greater water depths and open water (component 3).
Calling American toad presence was positively correlated with wetland area (component 4) and peatland habitat (component 6).
We conclude that wetland restoration by means of dredging accumulated organic soil and debris for the primary purpose of creating open water for waterfowl does provide habitat for anurans.
